column73: Identification (CE-A): The customer called requesting to bar his another number (01323185901) as lost phone. The agent sheared the number was inactive due to long-term non-usage and incorrectly instructed the customer to perform a round-figure recharge to reactivate the SIM before it could be barred. As per modality, the lost phone barring must be executed directly from CIM after proper verification. column76: The agent incorrectly informed the customer that the service cannot be deactivated ("???? ???? ???? ????? ???"), which directly contradicts the guideline stating that customers can indeed deactivate it. Furthermore, the agent recommended dialing *121*1005*3#, a code that activates both Before-Call (SoCN) and After-Call (EoCN) notifications; since the customer was already frustrated by receiving too many notifications, enabling additional ones aggravated the bad experience. The agent also misinformed the customer that this code would start flash pop-ups while the SMS notifications would continue. In Grameenphone's system, EoCN/EoDN are sent as USSD flash/pop-up messages by default rather than running as two separate concurrent services (SMS and Flash). The agent failed to follow the guideline matrix to properly deactivate the service.
